snp4.15:1.1: “When embraced, the rod of violence breeds danger & fear:
snp4.15:1.3: I will tell how I experienced terror:
snp4.15:2.4: as I saw this, fear came into me.
snp4.15:3.1: The world was entirely without substance.
snp4.15:3.2: All the directions were knocked out of line.
snp4.15:3.4: I saw nothing that wasn’t laid claim to.
snp4.15:4.1: Seeing nothing in the end but competition,
snp4.15:4.3: And then I saw an arrow here,
snp4.15:4.4: so very hard to see, embedded in the heart.
snp4.15:5.3: But simply on pulling it out
snp4.15:5.4: you don’t run, you don’t sink.
snp4.15:6.2: Whatever things are tied down in the world,
snp4.15:6.3: you shouldn’t be set on them.
snp4.15:6.4: Having totally penetrated sensual pleasures, sensual passions,
snp4.15:6.5: you should train for your own unbinding.
snp4.15:7.3: Without anger, the sage should cross over the evil
snp4.15:8.1: He should conquer drowsiness, weariness, sloth;
snp4.15:8.3: shouldn’t stand firm in his pride—
snp4.15:8.4: the man with his heart set on unbinding.
snp4.15:10.4: get entangled in what’s dazzling & bright.
snp4.15:11.1: I call greed a great flood;
snp4.15:11.4: sensuality, a bog hard to cross over.
snp4.15:12.2:
a sage stands on high ground :
a brahman.
snp4.15:12.4: he is said to be at peace;
snp4.15:13.1: having clearly known, he is an attainer-of-knowledge;
snp4.15:14.1: Whoever here has gone beyond sensual passions—
snp4.15:14.2: an attachment hard to transcend in the world—
snp4.15:14.4: He, his stream cut, is free from bonds.
snp4.15:15.3: If you don’t grasp at what’s in between,
snp4.15:16.1: For whom, in name-&-form, in every way,
snp4.15:16.3: and who doesn’t grieve over what is not:
snp4.15:16.4: He, in the world, isn’t defeated, suffers no loss.
snp4.15:17.1: To whom there doesn’t occur ‘This is mine,’
snp4.15:17.3: He, feeling no sense of mine-ness,
snp4.15:17.4: doesn’t grieve at the thought ‘I have nothing.’
snp4.15:18.3: This is the reward —I say when asked—
snp4.15:18.4: for those who are free from theorizing.
snp4.15:20.1: The sage doesn’t speak of himself as among those who are higher, equal, or lower.
snp4.15:20.4: he doesn’t embrace, doesn’t reject,” the Blessed One said.
